context: read a GitHub issue before fixing
action: Read issue title and body fully, identify exact symptom vs root cause, find mentioned file paths or function names
outcome: Clear understanding of what to change and where
belief: Issue title is often vague - the body contains the real signal

context: find relevant files for a bug fix
action: Grep for identifiers from the issue, narrow to 3-5 files, read them before writing any code
belief: Read before write - grep the issue keywords to find the right files

context: write a TypeScript fix or feature
action: Match existing code style exactly, preserve type signatures, add types for new code, no any
outcome: Code that passes pnpm typecheck without new errors
belief: TypeScript fixes must preserve or improve type safety - never use any

context: submit a pull request for open source
action: Reference the issue number, explain what changed and why, keep diff minimal and focused
belief: Minimal focused diffs get merged - big PRs get abandoned in review

context: pnpm build or typecheck fails after edit
action: Read the exact TypeScript error, trace the type mismatch, fix the type not the check
belief: TypeScript errors are precise - read them exactly, dont suppress with casts

context: tests fail after a code change
action: Run failing tests individually, read the assertion that failed, fix the code not the test
belief: Fix the code to match the test - never change tests to match broken code
